name: Paraiba (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the field, recalling Paraiba's revolutionary tradition
mourning for President-elect Joao Pessoa, assassinated in 1930

description: A horizontally divided flag, red over a black band along the bottom (one third), with the word NEGO in red capital letters on the black band.
history: Adopted in 1930 and officially recognised by State Law No. 4.488 of 26 August 1965, mourning the assassinated governor-elect Joao Pessoa, whose telegrammed reply 'NEGO' to political demands became the symbol of resistance leading to the 1930 Brazilian Revolution.

features.symbolism: A red field with a black band along the bottom bearing the single word NEGO in red capital letters
NEGO ('I refuse'), Joao Pessoa's defiant 'no' to political pressure from the central government in 1930, the act that contributed to his assassination and to the 1930 revolution
